era_context: Late Akkadian period; Gutian incursions ongoing. Egyptian Old Kingdom in its final phase; First Intermediate Period imminent. Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase. Traditional Xia Dynasty in early phase (chronology debated). Bronze Age established across Eurasia.

events.title: Akkadian Empire in advanced state of collapse
events.description: The Akkadian Empire is fragmenting into city-states and regional powers. Cuneiform records show minimal central authority. The Gutian invasions continue; some scholars place the final Akkadian collapse around this time, though the process is gradual. The transition from Akkadian to Gutian hegemony is not a single event but a process spanning several decades (c. 2150–2130 BCE).
events.certainty_note: The general process is well attested; specific year-by-year dating is uncertain.

events.title: Egyptian Old Kingdom final years; Sixth Dynasty near end
events.description: The Sixth Dynasty is in its final years. Central authority has largely collapsed; nomarchs rule independently. The transition to the First Intermediate Period (c. 2184–2055 BCE) is imminent. The exact date of the Sixth Dynasty's end is debated, but it falls within the range of c. 2184–2170 BCE.
events.certainty_note: The general decline is well documented; precise dating is uncertain (±20–30 years).

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: This year is essentially undocumented. No specific events are recorded. The entries represent the continuation of broader trends. Chronological uncertainty is substantial (±30–50 years).

graph_edges.note: Akkadian collapse leads to Gutian hegemony
